seo.title: Electronic Document Retention and Production - Working Group 1 | The Sedona Conference
seo.description: The mission of Working Group 1 is to develop principles, guidance and best practice recommendations for information governance and electronic discovery in the context of litigation, dispute resolution and investigations. The group released the first public comment draft of The Sedona Principles in March 2003, and the impact was immediate and substantial.

branding.title: Electronic Document Retention and Production

hero.title: Electronic Document Retention and Production
hero.description: The mission of Working Group 1 is to develop principles, guidance and best practice recommendations for information governance and electronic discovery in the context of litigation, dispute resolution and investigations. The group released the first public comment draft of The Sedona Principles in March 2003, and the impact was immediate and substantial.

items.label: Next meeting
items.description: WG1 will bring together experienced professionals to discuss recent case law on whether generative AI data may be protected by attorney-client privilege and work product doctrine.

items.label: Foundational work
items.description: The source page still centers WG1 around The Sedona Principles and notes the immediate impact of the March 2003 public comment draft.

items.label: Process guidance
items.description: WG1 currently surfaces publication style guidance plus brainstorming-team, drafting-team, liaison, and comment protocols for contributors.

activities.title: Current Activities
activities.description: The current WG1 page foregrounds mission, recent publication topics, and contributor guidance more than a named list of drafting tracks.

items.title: Information governance and electronic discovery
items.description: WG1's mission is to develop principles, guidance, and best practice recommendations for information governance and electronic discovery in litigation, dispute resolution, and investigations.

items.title: Mobile device and collaboration platform discovery
items.description: The 2025 publication list highlights commentary on mobile device data and collaboration platforms, reflecting WG1's ongoing work on modern communication evidence.

items.title: Internet of Things and database issues
items.description: Recent WG1 publications also address the electronic discovery implications of the Internet of Things and the handling of databases in civil discovery.

items.title: Publication drafting and comment protocols
items.description: WG1 currently surfaces publication style guidance plus brainstorming-team, drafting-team, liaison, and member-comment protocols for contributors.

resources.title: Resources
resources.description: WG1's source page combines membership access, recent publications, a publications-process announcement, and members-only reference notices.

cards.title: Join or Leave This Working Group
cards.description: Use the WG1 membership page to receive meeting notices, drafting team applications, draft-comment notices, and working group announcements.

cards.title: Working Group Publications Process
cards.description: This WG1 update links to publication style guidelines, brainstorming- and drafting-team leader guidance, liaison guidance, and member/public comment protocols.

cards.title: View All Working Group Publications
cards.description: The current page lists recent WG1 titles on mobile device data, the Internet of Things, collaboration platforms, judiciary resources, and databases.

cards.title: Publication Open for Public Comment
cards.description: The current WG1 page shows no publication open for public comment at this time.

cards.title: Members-only reference materials
cards.description: Reference materials are for working group members only and are not to be copied or republished.

aside.title: Sign in to unlock the full WG1 workspace
aside.description: Be sure to log in to view all content. Working group content access on the right side of the screen is disabled for non-members.

committee.title: Steering Committee

footer.description: The Sedona Conference is a nonpartisan, nonprofit research and educational institute dedicated to advancing law and policy through consensus-driven publications and legal education.
